content.content:
179	    /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
180	    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
181	    #[func]
182	    pub fn first(
183	        &self,
184	        /// A set of characters to strip from the start of the string before
185	        /// extracting the first grapheme cluster.
186	        #[named]
187	        strip: Option<Str>,
188	        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
189	        #[named]
190	        default: Option<Str>,
191	    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
192	        let s = match &strip {
193	            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_start_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
194	            None => self.0.as_str(),
195	        };
196	        s.graphemes(true)
197	            .next()
198	            .map(Into::into)
199	            .or(default)
200	            .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
201	    }
202	
203	    /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
204	    /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
205	    #[func]
206	    pub fn last(
207	        &self,
208	        /// A set of characters to strip from the end of the string before
209	        /// extracting the last grapheme cluster.
210	        #[named]
211	        strip: Option<Str>,
212	        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
213	        #[named]
214	        default: Option<Str>,
215	    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
216	        let s = match &strip {
217	            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_end_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
218	            None => self.0.as_str(),
219	        };
220	        s.graphemes(true)
221	            .next_back()
222	            .map(Into::into)
223	            .or(default)

diff --git a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
index d90b6f206..57ebbece5 100644
--- a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
+++ b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
@@ -179,22 +179,48 @@ impl Str {
     /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
-            .graphemes(true)
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the start of the string before
+        /// extracting the first grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let s = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_start_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),
+        };
+        s.graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
-            .graphemes(true)
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the end of the string before
+        /// extracting the last grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let s = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_end_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),
+        };
+        s.graphemes(true)
             .next_back()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }

content.text: Patch written to `patch.txt`. Both features are implemented in `str.rs`: `first()`/`last()` now take optional named `strip` and `default` parameters — `strip` trims matching leading/trailing characters before extraction, and `default` is used as a fallback via `.or(default)` when the (possibly stripped) string is empty. Existing no-argument calls remain fully compatible.

result: Patch written to `patch.txt`. Both features are implemented in `str.rs`: `first()`/`last()` now take optional named `strip` and `default` parameters — `strip` trims matching leading/trailing characters before extraction, and `default` is used as a fallback via `.or(default)` when the (possibly stripped) string is empty. Existing no-argument calls remain fully compatible.
